text: Robust multi-object tracking (MOT) in video sequences fundamentally depends on the ability to model complex spatial-temporal interactions between entities. In this work, we introduce TransMOT, a novel tracking framework that utilizes graph transformers to efficiently capture these relationships. By representing object trajectories as a series of sparse weighted graphs, TransMOT facilitates the effective modeling of interactions even across dense scenes. The architecture employs a specialized spatial graph transformer encoder, a temporal transformer encoder, and a spatial graph transformer decoder to process these representations. Our approach is not only more computationally efficient than traditional Transformer architectures but also yields superior tracking precision. To further optimize both speed and robustness, we introduce a cascade association mechanism designed to handle low-confidence detections and long-term occlusions—scenarios that typically demand excessive computational overhead. Experimental evaluations on the MOT15, MOT16, MOT17, and MOT20 benchmarks demonstrate that TransMOT achieves state-of-the-art performance across all datasets.
